methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

data.narrative:
The Exploration Company is a Series B-stage space and aerospace venture that has raised $160M to date. Valuation analysis draws from a peer set of three same-sector, same-stage private companies (Aalyria, Hubble Network, and Sift) with valuations ranging from $274M to $1.3B, supplemented by five public Space & Aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trading at a median 53.2x price-to-sales multiple.

Two quantitative methods proved applicable. The stage-progression approach, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $160M capital raised proxy, implies a $304M valuation. The peer-median method across the three same-stage Space & Aerospace companies yields $400M, the midpoint of a $274M–$1.3B range. Public-comp anchoring was not feasible given the absence of disclosed revenue figures for the target. These two methods triangulate to a range of $258M (low) to $460M (high), with a median implied valuation of $352M.

The widest uncertainty stems from the outsized $1.3B valuation of Aalyria within the peer set, creating material dispersion in the comparable universe. The absence of revenue data precludes calibration against public sector multiples. Additional clarity on growth trajectory and capital efficiency would refine the midpoint estimate.
